summary: Design the AgentTransport seam + frontier (OpenAI/Anthropic) tool-calling adapters so the in-app agent runs on frontier models, adversarially reviewed for security-invariant preservation

anthropicAdapter.requestBuild: New `buildAnthropicAgentBody(cfg, agentSystem, msgs: OllamaMsg[], schemas)` beside buildChatBody (after L206). URL `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`; headers EXACTLY buildChatBody anthropic case (verified L150): {'anthropic-version': ANTHROPIC_VERSION (='2023-06-01', ai-providers.ts:18), 'x-api-key': cfg.apiKey}. Body {model: cfg.model||DEFAULT_MODELS.anthropic, max_tokens: CHAT_MAX_TOKENS, stream:true, system: agentSystem, messages, tools}. KEEP buildChatBody's L151 omission — NO temperature/top_p/top_k/budget_tokens/thinking (verified comment: all 400 on fable-5/opus-4.8). tools = remap AGENT_TOOL_SCHEMAS (agent-tools.ts:53, OpenAI {type:'function',function:{name,description,parameters}}) → [{name: fn.function.name, description: fn.function.description, input_schema: fn.function.parameters}] (drop the {type:function} wrapper; rename parameters→input_schema). tools included ONLY in agent mode (this builder is agent-only; single-shot buildChatBody stays tool-free).
anthropicAdapter.messageTranslation: translateToAnthropic(msgs: OllamaMsg[]): agentSystem is top-level `system`, NEVER a message. Per OllamaMsg (role:'user'/'assistant'/'tool', content:string, tool_calls?, tool_name?). Maintain an in-adapter id map keyed by (turnIndex, callIndex). (1) assistant turn → {role:'assistant', content:[ ...(m.content ? [{type:'text',text:m.content}] : []), ...(m.tool_calls??[]).map((tc,i)=>{ const id = synthId(turnIndex,i); idForCall.set(key(turnIndex,i), id); return {type:'tool_use', id, name: tc.function.name, input: tc.function.arguments}; }) ]}. CRITICAL: if m.content==='' AND tool_calls present, OMIT the text block (Anthropic 400s on whitespace-only text alongside tool_use). (2) Consecutive role:'tool' result turns → GROUP into ONE {role:'user', content:[{type:'tool_result', tool_use_id: <id synthesized for the matching assistant tool_use, correlated BY ORDER>, content: t.content}]}. The loop appends exactly one role:'tool' per tool_call in tool_call order — VERIFIED: every branch in the L1268 loop (set_plan ack L1283, invoke_skill ack L1297/1301/1305/1312, unknown-tool L1318, recall-cap L1327, decline L1346, force-confirm L1350, real result L1390) pushes exactly one role:'tool' and `continue`s; there is no push-skipping path, so order-correlation is sound — but see sharedInvariants for the explicit pin that every L1268 iteration MUST emit exactly one role:'tool'. (3) role:'user' (real user turn) → {role:'user', content: m.content} (attachments already folded upstream into content). Because OllamaMsg has NO id, ids are synthesized per turn and remembered so tool_use.id === the following tool_result.tool_use_id. A mismatch 400s Anthropic.
anthropicAdapter.streamParser: New PURE parser `parseAnthropicAgentFrame(frame, state)` forked from parseAnthropicSse (L228, SSE '\n\n' framing via frameLines L217). State carried across frames (one StreamOnceResult per request): a Map<blockIndex,{id,name,argStr}> + a refusedNames[] list + a refusal flag. Events: (a) content_block_start w/ content_block.type==='tool_use' → state.blocks.set(index, {id: cb.id, name: cb.name, argStr: ''}). (b) content_block_delta w/ delta.type==='input_json_delta' → state.blocks.get(index).argStr += delta.partial_json. (c) content_block_delta w/ delta.type==='text_delta' → push delta.text live via onDelta + accumulate text EXACTLY as L257. (d) content_block_stop → apply finalizeToolCall (truncationGuard) to that index's buffer; emit on non-null, else record name into refusedNames. (e) message_delta delta.stop_reason: 'tool_use'/'end_turn'/'refusal'. refusal=true ONLY when stop_reason==='refusal' AND zero toolCalls accumulated (§4d mutual-exclusion). EXPLICIT: stop_reason==='tool_use' with ALL blocks refused → toolCalls.length===0, refusal STAYS false (it is a truncation-best-effort terminal, NOT a refusal); the loop succeeds on best-effort text. (f) message_stop → collapse into resolve (NOT a done field). (g) type==='error' → ChatStreamError with SAME categorization as L249-256 (overloaded_error/rate_limit_error→'rate-limited', authentication_error/permission_error→'key-missing', else 'generic'). Malformed JSON data line → skip, never throw (mirror L245-247). The parser FINALIZES at content_block_stop; the transport scaffold ALSO finalizes any still-open block on the response 'end' boundary (see truncationGuard — connection-drop guard) so a socket close mid-tool can never surface a raw buffer.

openaiAdapter.requestBuild: New `buildOpenAiAgentBody(cfg, agentSystem, msgs, schemas)` beside buildChatBody (after L206). URL `${OPENAI_BASE_URL}/chat/completions` (cfg.provider==='openai' → FIXED OPENAI_BASE_URL='https://api.openai.com/v1', NOT cfg.baseURL — verified L162 fixes base for the 'openai' case). Headers {authorization: `Bearer ${(cfg.apiKey??'').trim()}`} (verified L174). Body {model: cfg.model||DEFAULT_MODELS.openai, max_tokens: CHAT_MAX_TOKENS, stream:true, messages, tools}. tools = AGENT_TOOL_SCHEMAS AS-IS (already [{type:'function',function:{...}}] — pass through, NO remap). NO reasoning_effort (verified L171: that skipThinking branch is openai-compatible+local ONLY; real OpenAI rejects the value). messages leads with {role:'system', content: agentSystem} (L180 pattern) then the translated turns.
openaiAdapter.messageTranslation: translateToOpenAi(msgs): leading {role:'system', content: agentSystem}, then per OllamaMsg with the same in-adapter id map. (1) assistant turn → {role:'assistant', content: m.content || null, tool_calls: (m.tool_calls??[]).map((tc,i)=>{ const id = synthId(turnIndex,i); idForCall.set(...,id); return {id, type:'function', function:{name: tc.function.name, arguments: JSON.stringify(tc.function.arguments)}}; })}. content MUST be null (not '') when only tool_calls. (2) role:'tool' result turn → {role:'tool', tool_call_id: <id correlated BY ORDER to the matching assistant tool_call>, content: t.content}. OpenAI is ONE message per tool result (asymmetry vs Anthropic's grouped content[] array). (3) real user turn → {role:'user', content: m.content}. Same order-correlation + synthesized-id requirement; mismatch 400s OpenAI.
openaiAdapter.streamParser: New PURE `parseOpenAiAgentFrame(frame, state)` forked from parseOpenAiSse (L271, SSE '\n\n', `data: [DONE]`→done L279). State: Map<index,{id,name,argStr,sawArgFrag:boolean}> + refusedNames[]. choices[0].delta.tool_calls[]: FIRST fragment for an index carries {index, id, type:'function', function:{name}} → open buffer (capture id+name, sawArgFrag=false). SUBSEQUENT carry {index, function:{arguments:'<fragment>'}} → buffer.argStr += fragment; buffer.sawArgFrag = true. Accumulate STRICTLY by `index` (parallel tool_calls). delta.content (string, length>0) → text via onDelta + accumulate (L289-290). A chunk MAY carry content AND tool_calls together — handle both independently, NO mutual-exclusivity assumption. Terminal: choices[0].finish_reason 'tool_calls' (apply finalizeToolCall to every accumulated index) / 'stop' (terminal text) / 'length' (truncation — every open tool index runs finalizeToolCall which REFUSES any unterminated argStr). OpenAI has no native refusal → refusal stays false (optionally treat finish_reason==='content_filter' as terminal text, never a half-tool). [DONE]/finish_reason collapse into resolve, NOT a done field. CRITICAL OpenAI-specific guard (truncationGuard DEFECT-2 fix): an index with sawArgFrag===false (name frame arrived, ZERO argument fragments) means EITHER a legit no-arg tool OR a stream truncated between the name frame and the first arg fragment. OpenAI has NO content_block_start input:{} signal to disambiguate (unlike Anthropic). Resolution: argStr==='' is treated as {} ONLY when the static schema for `name` has NO required properties; if the tool's input_schema declares required args, REFUSE (return null). Error JSON / non-2xx handled by the shared scaffold's cat0 status mapping.

finalPlan.gateAndUi:
GATE (main): selectTransport's 3rd clause is the ONLY widening of the L698-702 gate. modelSupportsTools (ollama-manager.ts:243, async /api/tags probe) stays STRICTLY behind the local clause via `&&` short-circuit — a frontier agent call adds zero network latency. index.ts chat:send key-path already permits frontier-with-key: verified the rejection is `!cfg || (!cfg.apiKey && cfg.provider !== 'openai-compatible')` → a frontier provider that HAS a key is NOT rejected and reaches chatStream; a KEYLESS frontier provider is rejected with 'key-missing' BEFORE selectTransport (defense-in-depth — even if it reached selectTransport, the `(cfg.apiKey??'').trim()` clause returns null → single-shot). No handler change needed.

STATIC CAPABILITY TABLE — add to ai-providers.ts (verified NO electron import → ONE source for BOTH renderer agentCapable AND main selectTransport, keeping them in sync):
```ts
export function modelSupportsToolsStatic(provider: AiProvider, model: string): boolean {
  const m = (model || '').toLowerCase();
  if (provider === 'anthropic') return /claude-(3-5|3\.5|fable|opus|sonnet|haiku)/.test(m); // claude-3.5+/fable-5/opus/sonnet/haiku-4.x
  if (provider === 'openai')    return /^(gpt-4o|gpt-4\.1|o1|o3)/.test(m);                   // 4o / 4.1 / o-series
  return false; // google (Gemini) out of scope; openai-compatible uses the live probe, never this
}
```
Centralized so a future id only edits one place. Regex-drift acceptance (per critiques): too-narrow → silent single-shot (agent no-ops; annoying, not unsafe); too-broad → tools[] sent to a non-tool model → provider 400 (annoying, not unsafe). Neither breaches a security invariant. Matches MODELS_BY_PROVIDER ids (verified ai-providers.ts:70-71: claude-fable-5/opus-4-8/sonnet-4-6/haiku-4-5; gpt-4o/o3/o1/gpt-4.1).

P0-2 UI MIRROR (ChatView.tsx:156) — widen agentCapable from the local-only alias (verified `const agentCapable = canStartOllama`) to OR-in the frontier branch, reading the SAME static fn AND applying the SAME default-model resolution as main selectTransport (DEFECT-1 fix — `model || DEFAULT_MODELS[provider]`, NOT `model ?? ''`, so the empty-model fresh-config case does not mis-disable):
```ts
const agentCapable =
  canStartOllama ||
  ((ai?.provider === 'anthropic' || ai?.provider === 'openai')
    && !!ai?.hasKey                                                  // renderer sees a hasKey boolean (redact-secrets.ts:39), NEVER the key
    && modelSupportsToolsStatic(ai.provider, ai?.model || DEFAULT_MODELS[ai.provider]));
```
Import DEFAULT_MODELS + modelSupportsToolsStatic from ai-providers.ts (electron-free, renderer-safe). visionOn (verified L159 `= canStartOllama`) stays local-only — do NOT widen (frontier vision is a separate concern). chat:send already gates `agentOn: agentOn && agentCapable`, so widening agentCapable is what lets the renderer stop stripping agentOn for cloud — load-bearing, not cosmetic: without it main-side selectTransport never sees agentOn=true.

COMPOSER (Composer.tsx:212,222) — no structural change (keys off the agentCapable prop, verified). The 'local only' COPY (panel.ai.agentLocalOnly / agentLocalOnlyShort) becomes wrong once frontier enables. Re-key/re-word to a generic tools-capable-model message. i18n is en/ko ONLY (verified i18n.ts L9 `type Lang = 'en'|'ko'`, L10 `interface Entry { en; ko }`) — NOT 4 locales. Adding ja/zh would be a TYPE ERROR; the draft's '4 locales (en/ko/ja/zh)' is REJECTED. Update only en + ko.

RENDERER-OPACITY (unconditional): the renderer's ONLY input to selection is the persisted provider-id string + a hasKey BOOLEAN + model id. API keys/consent read main-side only (verified redact-secrets.ts: renderer gets hasKey, never key material). The renderer cannot enumerate/name/invoke/select tools. Frontier path preserves this — agentCapable consumes only provider/hasKey/model.
sharedInvariants: runAgentLoop consumes ONLY StreamOnceResult and calls streamStep with arity=1 (verified CE L1245) — NO per-provider branch inside the loop body; the loop is byte-identical for native vs frontier.

WRITE-CONFIRM GATE (verified CE L1341-1353): isWrite→onToolConfirm broker pause; forceConfirm (core_memory_*)→FAIL-CLOSED when no broker (L1349-1351). Fires identically for frontier; never auto-off for a 'smarter' model.
TOOL-NAME ALLOWLIST + REPAIR (verified CE L1316-1320, validNames + AGENT_MAX_INVALID=3): unknown name → invalidCount cap then succeed. Adapter must not bypass; renderer never names a tool.

DISPATCHER FIXED SWITCH by name + per-tool resolveInVault/assertInsideVault path-safety (agent-tools.ts L335-507, default→{error}). Args ALWAYS arrive as parsed objects (the parser's finalizeToolCall JSON.parse normalizes) — no string args leak to the dispatcher.

SINGLE-SETTLE: outer loop calls succeed/fail EXACTLY ONCE (terminal L1258/L1408). Adapters NEVER call outer succeed/fail — they only resolve, behind their OWN per-call `finished` guard mirroring streamOnceNative L1017-1018 ok/bad. A reject-after-resolve is an unhandled rejection the loop's try/catch at L1244-1246 cannot catch.
CAPS unconditional regardless of transport: AGENT_MAX_STEPS=12, AGENT_MAX_INVALID=3, AGENT_MAX_RECALL=4, AGENT_MAX_SKILL_INVOKE=2, DEAD_END_LIMIT=2.
drainSteer injection is role:'user' ONLY and ONLY at the top of the step (verified CE L1239-1241), never mid tool-batch — keeps assistant(tool_calls)↔role:'tool' alternation valid on every provider.

EVERY L1268 per-toolCall iteration MUST emit EXACTLY ONE role:'tool' message (verified: all 8 branches push-and-continue; none skip). This is what makes Anthropic/OpenAI order-correlation of tool_use↔tool_result ids sound. Pin it as an explicit invariant — a future `continue` that forgets the push would silently 400 the frontier providers (missing tool_result).

set_plan / invoke_skill are CONTROL tools intercepted BEFORE the allowlist (verified CE L1274/L1291), never dispatched, never settle — loop-local for all transports; they DO ride res.toolCalls and DO get one role:'tool' ack each, so id-correlation still holds.

INJECTION-SCAN is upstream (memory-store/skill-store); chat-engine consumes only pre-scanned coreMemory/skillCatalogue (verified system built at L692 before the agent branch). Adapters add NO new untrusted-text path into the trusted system role — agentSystem is the same frozen snapshot for native and frontier.

RENDERER-OPACITY: the only renderer input to selection is the persisted provider-id string + hasKey boolean + model id. API keys/consent read main-side only (verified redact-secrets.ts:39); renderer cannot enumerate/invoke/select tools or see keys.

§4d REFUSAL MUTUAL-EXCLUSION: res.refusal→fail() is immediate (verified L1255). Adapter emits refusal:true ONLY on a terminal turn with empty toolCalls AND a true refusal stop_reason — never when a tool_use/tool_call block was accumulated, and never for stop_reason='tool_use' with all-refused blocks (that is best-effort terminal succeed, refusal:false) — so no half-built write reaches messages[] before fail.
steps: ai-providers.ts (after L74, beside MODELS_BY_PROVIDER / isLocalProviderUrl) — add `modelSupportsToolsStatic(provider, model): boolean` (anthropic /claude-(3-5|3\.5|fable|opus|sonnet|haiku)/; openai /^(gpt-4o|gpt-4\.1|o1|o3)/; google/openai-compatible/none → false). ONE source for renderer + main. No electron import (verified file is electron-free).
chat-engine.ts — add `AgentTransport` interface (verbatim §6.6 §2) beside StreamOnceResult (after L970).

chat-engine.ts — add `buildAnthropicAgentBody(cfg, agentSystem, msgs, schemas)` + `translateToAnthropic(msgs)` (id-map, grouped tool_result, omit empty text block) after L206. Remap AGENT_TOOL_SCHEMAS parameters→input_schema, drop {type:function}. Keep L151 omissions (no temperature/thinking).

chat-engine.ts — add `buildOpenAiAgentBody(cfg, agentSystem, msgs, schemas)` + `translateToOpenAi(msgs)` (id-map, content||null, one role:'tool' per result, leading system msg, fixed OPENAI_BASE_URL not cfg.baseURL, no reasoning_effort). tools pass-through.

chat-engine.ts — add PURE `parseAnthropicAgentFrame(frame, state)` + `parseOpenAiAgentFrame(frame, state)` forking parseAnthropicSse/parseOpenAiSse; accumulate tool-call args per block-index/index; embed `finalizeToolCall` with the '' → {} ONLY-when-no-required-args rule, the Array.isArray reject, and the unterminated/parse-fail REFUSE; map error/refusal; collapse terminal into resolve. State carries refusedNames + per-index sawArgFrag (OpenAI).

chat-engine.ts — add `streamOnceFrontier(provider, url, headers, body, signal, onDelta, parseFrame, finalizeOpenBuffers)` cloning streamOnceNative L983-1124 lifecycle BUT: SSE '

' framing (split on blank-line boundary via frameLines, not '
' NDJSON); `redirect:'manual'` on net.request + assert final host (api.anthropic.com / api.openai.com, case-insensitive) BEFORE attaching the key header — verified BOTH net.request sites (L999, single-shot streamer) set NO redirect option so Electron defaults to 'follow', and key headers attach before send → a 3xx replays the key; manual-redirect+host-assert is a genuine fix; per-call `finished` single-resolve guard; cat0 status mapping; ON 'end' run finalizeOpenBuffers (REFUSE any still-open block) before ok() — NOT the unconditional L1106 copy; ON onAbort resolve toolCalls:[] (drop open buffers) — NOT the L1022 accumulator copy.

chat-engine.ts — add `makeNativeAdapter` (wrap current L742-743 thunk, zero behavior change) + `makeFrontierAdapter(provider, cfg, toolset, agentSystem, signal, onDelta)` (closure-bind signal/onDelta; pick body builder + parser by provider; call streamOnceFrontier; resolve StreamOnceResult).

chat-engine.ts — add `async selectTransport(cfg, opts, agentSystem, signal, onDelta): Promise<AgentTransport|null>` generalizing the L698-702 gate (injected-deps → native(local+probe, short-circuited) → frontier(provider+key+static) → null). Use `cfg.model || DEFAULT_MODELS[cfg.provider]`.

chat-engine.ts L716-758 — build `agentSystem` in the SAME 3-way branch but BEFORE the gate; replace the inline `if(...){...streamStep: thunk...}` block with `const transport = await selectTransport(cfg, opts, agentSystem, signal, onDelta); if(transport){ await runAgentLoop({...ctx, streamStep:(msgs)=>transport.streamStep(msgs)}); return; }` then fall through to L760 single-shot when null.

ChatView.tsx:156 — widen `agentCapable = canStartOllama || ((provider==='anthropic'||provider==='openai') && !!ai?.hasKey && modelSupportsToolsStatic(ai.provider, ai?.model || DEFAULT_MODELS[ai.provider]))`. Import modelSupportsToolsStatic + DEFAULT_MODELS. Leave visionOn (L159) local-only.
Composer.tsx:212,222 — no logic change (keys off agentCapable prop); re-key the hint copy from 'local only' to a tools-capable-model message.

i18n.ts — re-word `panel.ai.agentLocalOnly` / `agentLocalOnlyShort` (or add `agentUnavailable` / `agentUnavailableShort`) for en + ko ONLY (verified the file is en/ko, type Lang='en'|'ko', interface Entry{en;ko}). DO NOT add ja/zh — those fields do not exist on the Entry type and would not compile.
testPlan: UNIT parseAnthropicAgentFrame: (a) content_block_start tool_use + 2x input_json_delta + content_block_stop → one OllamaToolCall with parsed OBJECT args; (b) text_delta still streams via onDelta; (c) message_delta stop_reason='tool_use' vs 'end_turn' vs 'refusal' (refusal:true ONLY when stop_reason='refusal' AND no toolCalls); (d) malformed data line skipped, never throws; (e) type='error' → ChatStreamError with correct category (overloaded_error→rate-limited, authentication_error→key-missing).

UNIT parseOpenAiAgentFrame: (a) first fragment {index,id,function:{name}} + arg fragments concatenated by index → object args; (b) TWO parallel tool_calls indices accumulated independently (no cross-block corruption); (c) chunk carrying content AND tool_calls together handled both; (d) finish_reason 'tool_calls' vs 'stop'; [DONE] ends; (e) finish_reason 'length' mid-arg → SKIP.

UNIT finalizeToolCall (BOTH providers): mid-object argStr ('{"a":1' no closing brace) → null (SKIP); valid '{}' with no-required-args tool → {}; '[1,2]' → REFUSE (Array.isArray reject — assert the draft's bare typeof check would WRONGLY pass); JSON.parse-throwing garbage → null; argStr='' with a no-required-args tool → {}; argStr='' with a REQUIRED-args tool (OpenAI name-only truncation) → null (DEFECT-2 case). Assert a truncated core_memory_replace NEVER yields arguments:{} on EITHER the unterminated path OR the name-only-truncation path (the load-bearing cases).

UNIT abort/end-boundary guards: (FLAW-1) onAbort with a half-accumulated block buffer → resolves toolCalls:[] (NOT the raw accumulator); (DEFECT-3) response 'end' WITHOUT a terminal frame, one block open mid-input_json_delta → that block REFUSED (or 'generic' error), never a raw {name,argStr} in toolCalls.

UNIT translateToAnthropic: assistant{content,tool_calls}→content blocks with synthesized ids; empty content + tool_use → text block OMITTED; consecutive role:'tool' grouped into ONE user content[] with tool_use_id matching the prior tool_use id (order-correlation); 2-tool-call turn → 2 tool_use + 2 tool_result, ids paired.
UNIT translateToOpenAi: assistant content''→null; tool_calls arguments JSON.stringify'd; each role:'tool'→one message with tool_call_id matching by order; leading system message present.

UNIT (FLAW-2) one-emitted + one-refused tool call in a turn → next request: the assistant turn carries ONLY the emitted tool_call, exactly one matching tool_result, NO orphan tool_use (asserts the §6.6 skip lives in the parser, upstream of L1260; would Anthropic-400 if the skip were in the translator).

UNIT modelSupportsToolsStatic: claude-fable-5/opus-4-8/sonnet-4-6/haiku-4-5 true; gpt-4o/gpt-4.1/o3/o1 true; gemini-2.0-flash false; llama3.1 false; empty-string model false (asserts the UI mirror's `model || DEFAULT_MODELS` default-resolve is needed — DEFECT-1).

UNIT selectTransport clause order: agentOn=false→null; local+probe-true→native; anthropic+key+static-true→frontier; anthropic+NO key→null; openai+key+unsupported-model→null; google+key→null; anthropic+key+EMPTY-model→frontier (default resolves to claude-fable-5). Assert modelSupportsTools (live probe) is NOT called on any frontier branch.

SMOKE (extend smoke15→smoke16): frontier happy-path agent — mocked Anthropic SSE tool_use→tool_result→end_turn round-trips through runAgentLoop with the confirm gate firing on a write; native happy-path regression unchanged.
SMOKE single-settle: redirect-mid-stream and abort-mid-stream each resolve EXACTLY ONCE with no later reject (unhandled-rejection assertion); HTTP 429 → rate-limited category once.

GATE/UI mirror test: ipc-security-chat — agentOn stripped to false when provider is frontier WITHOUT key or unsupported model; agentOn preserved when frontier+key+supported (mirrors ChatView agentCapable incl. empty-model default-resolve). Assert renderer payload never contains the API key (hasKey boolean only).
INTEGRATION outbound hardening: a 3xx Location off api.anthropic.com/api.openai.com → request abort, key header NOT sent to the redirect host (final-host assertion fires before header attach).
finalPlan.scopeCuts: ["NO Track-B/Responses pieces: no OAuth/consent/token gating, no chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex endpoint, no wire_api='responses' body (instructions+input[]+store:false), no CAPTURE-REQUIRED event names (response.output_text.delta/response.completed/failed), no live-capture HARD GATE. Standard frontier uses api.anthropic.com/v1/messages + api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions with standard SSE — reuse §6.6 assembly + truncation guard, NONE of §4's OAuth/Responses wire.", "NO Gemini/google tool-calling adapter (second-brain Phase 2; modelSupportsToolsStatic returns false for google — stays single-shot).", "NO parallel/multi-worker tool execution — loop stays serial dispatch (DEFERRED §7); single-settle stays simple.", "NO change to the single-shot SSE streamer (buildChatBody text path, parseAnthropicSse/parseOpenAiSse) — frontier AGENT is additive; the text-only single-shot path is untouched (no temperature/thinking regression).", "NO new IPC channels — frontier rides the existing chat:send path; renderer adds only the widened agentCapable read (provider/hasKey/model already available via redact-secrets).", "NO frontier vision (visionOn stays local-only, verified L159) — out of scope for this seam.", "NO network capability probe for frontier — static table only; modelSupportsTools (ollama probe) stays strictly behind the local clause via && short-circuit.", "NO ja/zh i18n — the i18n module is en/ko only (type Lang='en'|'ko'); the draft's '4 locales' claim is REJECTED as it would not compile. Edit en + ko only.", "DEFER reflection auto-propose aggressiveness and FTS5 session recall (§7) — not security invariants, not built here."]

anthropicAdapter.requestBuild: New `buildAnthropicAgentBody(cfg, agentSystem, msgs: OllamaMsg[], schemas)` beside buildChatBody L139. URL `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`; headers EXACTLY buildChatBody anthropic case L150: {'anthropic-version': ANTHROPIC_VERSION, 'x-api-key': cfg.apiKey}. Body {model, max_tokens: CHAT_MAX_TOKENS, stream:true, system: agentSystem, messages, tools}. KEEP buildChatBody's L151 omission — NO temperature/top_p/thinking/budget_tokens (400 on fable-5/opus). tools = remap AGENT_TOOL_SCHEMAS (agent-tools.ts:53, OpenAI {type:'function',function:{name,description,parameters}}) → [{name: fn.function.name, description: fn.function.description, input_schema: fn.function.parameters}] (drop the {type:function} wrapper; rename parameters→input_schema). Include tools ONLY in agent mode (this builder is agent-only).
anthropicAdapter.messageTranslation: translateToAnthropic(msgs: OllamaMsg[]): the agentSystem is top-level `system`, NOT a message. Per OllamaMsg: role:'user'/'assistant'/'tool'. Maintain an in-adapter id map. (1) assistant turn → {role:'assistant', content:[ ...(m.content ? [{type:'text',text:m.content}] : []), ...(m.tool_calls??[]).map((tc,i)=>{ const id = synthId(turnIndex,i); idForCall.set(key(turnIndex,i), id); return {type:'tool_use', id, name: tc.function.name, input: tc.function.arguments}; }) ]}. CRITICAL: if m.content==='' AND tool_calls present, OMIT the text block (Anthropic 400s on whitespace-only text alongside tool_use). (2) Consecutive role:'tool' result turns → GROUP into ONE {role:'user', content:[{type:'tool_result', tool_use_id: <the id synthesized for the matching assistant tool_use, correlated BY ORDER>, content: t.content}]}. The loop appends exactly one role:'tool' per tool_call in tool_call order (CE L1268,L1390), so order-correlation is sound. (3) role:'user' (real user turn) → {role:'user', content: m.content} (fold attachments already done upstream at L1209). Because OllamaMsg has NO id, ids are synthesized per turn and remembered so tool_use.id === the following tool_result.tool_use_id. A mismatch 400s Anthropic.
anthropicAdapter.streamParser: New PURE parser `parseAnthropicAgentFrame(frame, state)` forked from parseAnthropicSse L228 (SSE '\n\n' framing). State carried across frames (one StreamOnceResult per request): a Map<blockIndex,{id,name,argStr}>. Events: (a) content_block_start w/ content_block.type==='tool_use' → open buffer state.blocks.set(index, {id: cb.id, name: cb.name, argStr: ''}). (b) content_block_delta w/ delta.type==='input_json_delta' → state.blocks.get(index).argStr += delta.partial_json. (c) content_block_delta w/ delta.type==='text_delta' → push delta.text (live via onDelta + accumulate text) EXACTLY as L257. (d) content_block_stop → apply §6.6 guard (see truncationGuard) to that index's argStr, emit-or-skip. (e) message_delta delta.stop_reason: 'tool_use' (has toolCalls) / 'end_turn' (terminal text) / 'refusal' → refusal=true ONLY if no toolCalls accumulated (§4d mutual-exclusion). (f) message_stop → done (collapsed into resolve, NOT a done field). (g) type==='error' → ChatStreamError with the SAME categorization as L249-256 (overloaded/rate_limit→'rate-limited', authentication/permission→'key-missing', else 'generic'). Malformed JSON → skip line, never throw (mirror L245-247). Refusal stop_reason categorization mirrors L259.

openaiAdapter.requestBuild: New `buildOpenAiAgentBody(cfg, agentSystem, msgs, schemas)` beside buildChatBody L160. URL `${OPENAI_BASE_URL}/chat/completions` (cfg.provider==='openai' → fixed OPENAI_BASE_URL, NOT cfg.baseURL). Headers {authorization: `Bearer ${(cfg.apiKey??'').trim()}`} (L174). Body {model, max_tokens: CHAT_MAX_TOKENS, stream:true, messages, tools}. tools = AGENT_TOOL_SCHEMAS AS-IS (already [{type:'function',function:{...}}] — pass through, no remap). NO reasoning_effort (that L171 skipThinking branch is local-only; frontier OpenAI rejects it). messages leads with {role:'system', content: agentSystem} (L180 pattern) then the translated turns.
openaiAdapter.messageTranslation: translateToOpenAi(msgs): leading {role:'system', content: agentSystem}, then per OllamaMsg with the same in-adapter id map. (1) assistant turn → {role:'assistant', content: m.content || null, tool_calls: (m.tool_calls??[]).map((tc,i)=>{ const id = synthId(turnIndex,i); idForCall.set(...,id); return {id, type:'function', function:{name: tc.function.name, arguments: JSON.stringify(tc.function.arguments)}}; })}. content MUST be null (not '') when only tool_calls. (2) role:'tool' result turn → {role:'tool', tool_call_id: <id correlated BY ORDER to the matching assistant tool_call>, content: t.content}. OpenAI is ONE message per tool result (asymmetry vs Anthropic's grouped content[] array). (3) real user turn → {role:'user', content: m.content}. Same order-correlation + synthesized-id requirement; mismatch 400s OpenAI.
openaiAdapter.streamParser: New PURE `parseOpenAiAgentFrame(frame, state)` forked from parseOpenAiSse L271 (SSE '\n\n', `data: [DONE]` → done L279). State: Map<index,{id,name,argStr}>. choices[0].delta.tool_calls[] — FIRST fragment for an index carries {index, id, type:'function', function:{name}} → open buffer (capture id+name). SUBSEQUENT carry {index, function:{arguments:'<fragment>'}} → state.get(index).argStr += fragment. Accumulate STRICTLY by `index` (parallel tool_calls). delta.content (string) → text via onDelta + accumulate (L289). A chunk may carry content AND tool_calls together — handle both independently, no mutual-exclusivity assumption. Terminal: choices[0].finish_reason 'tool_calls' (apply §6.6 guard to every accumulated index, emit-or-skip) / 'stop' (terminal text) / 'length' (truncation — the §6.6 attack surface; any open tool index whose argStr is not }/]-terminated → SKIP). OpenAI has no native refusal → refusal stays false (optionally detect finish_reason==='content_filter' and treat as terminal text, not a half-tool). [DONE]/finish_reason collapse into resolve, NOT a done field. Error JSON / non-2xx handled by the shared lifecycle's cat0 status mapping (reuse streamOnceNative scaffold).

draft.gateAndUi:
GATE (main): selectTransport's 3rd clause (above) is the ONLY widening of the L698-702 gate. modelSupportsTools (ollama-manager.ts:243, async /api/tags probe) stays STRICTLY behind the local clause — frontier uses the STATIC table only, so a frontier agent call adds zero network latency. index.ts chat:send key-path already permits frontier-with-key (`!cfg.apiKey && provider !== 'openai-compatible'` rejection does NOT reject a frontier provider that HAS a key) — verified; no handler change needed there.

STATIC CAPABILITY TABLE — add to ai-providers.ts:28 (no electron import → ONE source for BOTH renderer agentCapable AND main selectTransport, keeping them in sync):
```ts
export function modelSupportsToolsStatic(provider: AiProvider, model: string): boolean {
  const m = (model || '').toLowerCase();
  if (provider === 'anthropic') return /claude-(3-5|3\.5|fable|opus|sonnet|haiku)/.test(m); // claude-3.5+/fable-5/opus/sonnet/haiku-4.x
  if (provider === 'openai')    return /^(gpt-4o|gpt-4\.1|o1|o3)/.test(m);                   // 4o / 4.1 / o-series
  return false; // google (Gemini) out of scope (second-brain Phase 2); openai-compatible uses the live probe, never this
}
```
Permissive-enough to avoid silent single-shot fallback on current ids; centralized so a future id only edits one place.

P0-2 UI MIRROR (ChatView.tsx:156) — widen agentCapable from the local-only alias to OR-in the frontier branch, reading the SAME static fn:
```ts
const agentCapable =
  canStartOllama ||
  ((ai?.provider === 'anthropic' || ai?.provider === 'openai')
    && !!ai?.hasKey                                        // renderer sees a hasKey boolean, NEVER the key
    && modelSupportsToolsStatic(ai.provider, ai?.model ?? ''));
```
visionOn (L159) stays `= canStartOllama` (frontier vision is a separate concern — do NOT widen it). chat:send L480 already gates `agentOn: agentOn && agentCapable`, so widening agentCapable is what lets the renderer stop stripping agentOn for cloud — this is load-bearing, not cosmetic: without it main-side selectTransport never sees agentOn=true.

COMPOSER (Composer.tsx:202-224) — no structural change (it keys off the agentCapable prop). The 'local only' COPY becomes wrong once frontier enables: re-key the title/short hints. agentLocalOnly → a generic "needs a tools-capable model" message; agentLocalOnlyShort → drop or re-word. Update across i18n.ts en/ko/ja/zh (4 locales). New keys e.g. panel.ai.agentUnavailable / agentUnavailableShort so the pill on a keyless/unsupported cloud model reads correctly.

RENDERER-OPACITY (unconditional): the renderer's ONLY input to selection is the persisted provider-id string + a hasKey BOOLEAN + model id. API keys/consent are read main-side only. The renderer cannot enumerate/name/invoke/select tools. Frontier path preserves this — agentCapable consumes only provider/hasKey/model.
sharedInvariants: runAgentLoop consumes ONLY StreamOnceResult and calls streamStep with arity=1 (CE L1245) — NO per-provider branch inside the loop body; the loop is byte-identical for native vs frontier.
WRITE-CONFIRM GATE (CE L1341-1353): isWrite→onToolConfirm broker pause; forceConfirm (core_memory_*)→FAIL-CLOSED when no broker. Fires identically for frontier; never auto-off for a 'smarter' model.
TOOL-NAME ALLOWLIST + REPAIR (CE L1316-1320, validNames + AGENT_MAX_INVALID=3): unknown name → invalidCount cap then succeed. Adapter must not bypass; renderer never names a tool.

DISPATCHER FIXED SWITCH by name + per-tool resolveInVault/assertInsideVault path-safety (agent-tools.ts L335-507, default→{error}). Args ALWAYS arrive as parsed objects — no string args leak to the dispatcher.

SINGLE-SETTLE: outer loop calls succeed/fail EXACTLY ONCE (CE L658-668, terminal L1258/L1408). Adapters NEVER call outer succeed/fail — they only resolve, behind their OWN per-call `finished` guard (mirror streamOnceNative L1017-1018 ok/bad). A reject-after-resolve is an unhandled rejection the loop's try/catch at L1244 cannot catch.
CAPS unconditional regardless of transport: AGENT_MAX_STEPS=12, AGENT_MAX_INVALID=3, AGENT_MAX_RECALL=4, AGENT_MAX_SKILL_INVOKE=2, DEAD_END_LIMIT=2.
drainSteer injection is role:'user' ONLY and ONLY at the top of the step (CE L1239-1241), never mid tool-batch — keeps assistant(tool_calls)↔role:'tool' alternation valid on every provider.
set_plan / invoke_skill are CONTROL tools intercepted BEFORE the allowlist (CE L1274/L1291), never dispatched, never settle — loop-local for all transports.

INJECTION-SCAN is upstream (memory-store/skill-store/index.ts); chat-engine consumes only pre-scanned coreMemory/skillCatalogue (CE L692, L716). Adapters add NO new untrusted-text path into the trusted system role — agentSystem is the same frozen snapshot for native and frontier.

RENDERER-OPACITY: the only renderer input to selection is the persisted provider-id string + hasKey boolean + model id. API keys/consent read main-side only; renderer cannot enumerate/invoke/select tools or see keys.

§4d REFUSAL MUTUAL-EXCLUSION: res.refusal→fail() is immediate (L1255). Adapter emits refusal:true ONLY on a terminal turn with empty toolCalls — never when a tool_use/tool_call block was accumulated — so no half-built write reaches messages[] before fail.
steps: ai-providers.ts:28 — add `modelSupportsToolsStatic(provider, model): boolean` (anthropic claude-3.5+/fable/opus/sonnet/haiku; openai gpt-4o*/gpt-4.1*/o1/o3; google/openai-compatible → false). ONE source for renderer + main.
chat-engine.ts — add `AgentTransport` interface (verbatim §6.6 §2) beside StreamOnceResult (after L970).

chat-engine.ts — add `buildAnthropicAgentBody(cfg, agentSystem, msgs, schemas)` + `translateToAnthropic(msgs)` (id-map, grouped tool_result, omit empty text block) beside buildChatBody (after L206). Remap AGENT_TOOL_SCHEMAS parameters→input_schema, drop {type:function}.
chat-engine.ts — add `buildOpenAiAgentBody(cfg, agentSystem, msgs, schemas)` + `translateToOpenAi(msgs)` (id-map, content||null, one role:'tool' per result, leading system msg). tools pass-through.

chat-engine.ts — add PURE `parseAnthropicAgentFrame(frame, state)` + `parseOpenAiAgentFrame(frame, state)` forking parseAnthropicSse/parseOpenAiSse; accumulate tool-call args per block-index/index; embed `finalizeToolCall` §6.6 guard; map error/refusal/done.

chat-engine.ts — add `streamOnceFrontier(provider, url, headers, body, signal, onDelta, parseFrame)` cloning streamOnceNative L983-1124 lifecycle BUT: SSE '

' framing (split on blank-line boundary, not '
' NDJSON), `redirect:'manual'` on net.request + assert final host (api.anthropic.com / api.openai.com, case-insensitive) BEFORE attaching the key header (SP0 outbound hardening — default redirect:'follow' is a key-exfil vector), per-call `finished` single-resolve guard, cat0 status mapping, collapse terminal frame into resolve (no done field).

chat-engine.ts — add `makeNativeAdapter` (wrap current L742-743 thunk, zero behavior change) + `makeFrontierAdapter(provider, cfg, toolset, agentSystem, signal, onDelta)` (closure-bind signal/onDelta; pick body builder + parser by provider; call streamOnceFrontier; resolve StreamOnceResult).

chat-engine.ts — add `async selectTransport(cfg, opts, agentSystem, signal, onDelta): Promise<AgentTransport|null>` generalizing the L698-702 gate (injected-deps → native(local+probe) → frontier(provider+key+static) → null).

chat-engine.ts L698-758 — build `agentSystem` in the SAME 3-way branch but BEFORE the gate; replace the inline `if(...){...streamStep: thunk...}` block with `const transport = await selectTransport(cfg, opts, agentSystem, signal, onDelta); if(transport){ await runAgentLoop({...ctx, streamStep:(msgs)=>transport.streamStep(msgs)}); return; }` then fall through to L760 single-shot when null.
ChatView.tsx:156 — widen `agentCapable = canStartOllama || (frontier provider && hasKey && modelSupportsToolsStatic(provider, model))`. Leave visionOn local-only.
Composer.tsx:202-224 — no logic change (keys off agentCapable prop); re-key the hint copy from 'local only' to a tools-capable-model message.

i18n.ts — re-word panel.ai.agentLocalOnly / agentLocalOnlyShort (or add agentUnavailable / agentUnavailableShort) across en/ko/ja/zh so the disabled-pill hint is correct for keyless/unsupported cloud models.
testPlan: UNIT parseAnthropicAgentFrame: (a) content_block_start tool_use + 2× input_json_delta + content_block_stop → one OllamaToolCall with parsed OBJECT args; (b) text_delta still streams via onDelta; (c) message_delta stop_reason='tool_use' vs 'end_turn' vs 'refusal' (refusal only when no toolCalls); (d) malformed data line skipped, never throws; (e) type='error' → ChatStreamError with correct category (overloaded→rate-limited, authentication→key-missing).

UNIT parseOpenAiAgentFrame: (a) first fragment {index,id,function:{name}} + arg fragments concatenated by index → object args; (b) TWO parallel tool_calls indices accumulated independently (no cross-block corruption); (c) chunk carrying content AND tool_calls together handled both; (d) finish_reason 'tool_calls' vs 'stop'; [DONE] ends; (e) finish_reason 'length' mid-arg → SKIP.

UNIT §6.6 finalizeToolCall (BOTH providers): mid-object args string ('{"a":1' no closing brace) → null (SKIP); valid '{}'→{}; '[1,2]'→array rejected as non-object→null; JSON.parse-throwing garbage → null; argStr==='' (no-arg tool) → {}. Assert a truncated core_memory_replace NEVER yields arguments:{} (the load-bearing case).

UNIT translateToAnthropic: assistant{content,tool_calls}→content blocks with synthesized ids; empty content + tool_use → text block OMITTED; consecutive role:'tool' grouped into ONE user content[] with tool_use_id matching the prior tool_use id (order-correlation); 2-tool-call turn → 2 tool_use + 2 tool_result, ids paired.
UNIT translateToOpenAi: assistant content''→null; tool_calls arguments JSON.stringify'd; each role:'tool'→one message with tool_call_id matching by order; leading system message present.
UNIT modelSupportsToolsStatic: claude-fable-5/opus-4-8/sonnet-4-6/haiku-4-5 true; gpt-4o/gpt-4.1/o3/o1 true; gemini-2.0-flash false; llama3.1 false (uses live probe path, not this).

UNIT selectTransport clause order: agentOn=false→null; local+probe-true→native; anthropic+key+static-true→frontier; anthropic+NO key→null; openai+key+unsupported-model→null; google+key→null. Assert modelSupportsTools (live probe) is NOT called on the frontier branch.

SMOKE (extend smoke15): frontier happy-path agent — mocked Anthropic SSE tool_use→tool_result→end_turn round-trips through runAgentLoop with confirm gate firing on a write; native happy-path regression unchanged.
SMOKE single-settle: redirect-mid-stream and abort-mid-stream each resolve EXACTLY ONCE with no later reject (unhandled-rejection assertion); HTTP 429 → rate-limited category once.

GATE/UI mirror test: ipc-security-chat — agentOn stripped to false when provider is frontier WITHOUT key or unsupported model; agentOn preserved when frontier+key+supported (mirrors ChatView agentCapable). Assert renderer payload never contains the API key.
INTEGRATION outbound hardening: a 3xx Location off api.anthropic.com/api.openai.com → request abort, key header NOT sent to the redirect host (final-host assertion fires before header attach).
draft.scopeCuts: ["NO Track-B/Responses pieces: no OAuth/consent/token gating, no chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex endpoint, no wire_api='responses' body (instructions+input[]+store:false), no CAPTURE-REQUIRED event names (response.output_text.delta/response.completed/failed), no live-capture HARD GATE. Standard frontier uses api.anthropic.com/v1/messages + api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions with standard SSE — reuse §4(a) assembly + §4(b) guard, NONE of §4's OAuth/Responses wire.","NO Gemini/google tool-calling adapter (second-brain Phase 2; modelSupportsToolsStatic returns false for google — stays single-shot).","NO parallel/multi-worker tool execution — loop stays serial dispatch (DEFERRED §7); single-settle stays simple.","NO change to the single-shot SSE streamer (buildChatBody text path, parseAnthropicSse/parseOpenAiSse) — frontier AGENT is additive; the text-only single-shot path is untouched (no temperature/thinking regression).","NO new IPC channels — frontier rides the existing chat:send path; renderer adds only the widened agentCapable read (provider/hasKey/model already available).","NO frontier vision (visionOn stays local-only) — out of scope for this seam.","NO network capability probe for frontier — static table only; modelSupportsTools (ollama probe) stays strictly behind the local clause.","DEFER reflection auto-propose aggressiveness and FTS5 session recall (§7) — not security invariants, not built here."]
